STIPULATION AND ORDER FOR MOTION TO BE HEARD BY A MEMBER OF THE CALIFORNIA BAR SERVING AS A TEMPORARY JUDGE

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT HEAGY'S MOTION TO QUASH THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO REAL ESTATE SOURCE. THE MOTION TO QUASH IS DENIED. THE DOCUMENTS REQUESTED MAY CONTAIN POTENTIALLY RELEVANT EVIDENCE. THE EXTENT TO WHICH THE DOCUMENTS REFER TO THE STATUS OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTY MORE THAN A YEAR AFTER THE PLAINTIFFS LEFT GOES TO THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE, NOT ITS POTENTIAL ADMISSIBILITY. IT IS HARD FOR THE COURT TO DETERMINE WHAT FINANCIAL INFORMATION IN THE DOCUMENTS IS NOT ALREADY KNOWN THROUGH PUBLIC RECORDS. HOWEVER, THE RIGHT TO PRIVACY IS OUTWEIGHED BY THE NECESSITY TO REVIEW THE EVIDENCE. THE COURT WILL RETAIN JURISDICTION TO REVIEW, IN CAMERA, ANY DOCUMENTS ALLEGED TO CONTAIN FINANCIAL INFORMATION. ORDER SIGN IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM THOMAS COHEN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Real Estate Source
Off Calendar Nov-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Mary C. Wiederhold of Law Offices of Mary C. Wiederhold 415-533-0735 appeared for plaintiff. No other appearances.

Hearing Held - The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ROBERT HEAGY'S Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Real Estate Source. The motion to quash is denied. The documents requested may contain potentially relevant evidence. The extent to which the documents refer to the status of the subject property more than a year after the plaintiffs left goes to the weight of the evidence, not its potential admissibility. It is hard for the court to determine what financial information in the documents is not already known through public records. However, the right to privacy is outweighed by the necessity to review the evidence. The court will retain jurisdiction to review, in camera, any documents alleged to contain financial information. Order sign in open court. Judge Pro Tem Thomas Cohen, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Commpel Defendant Robert Heagys Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctons Against Defendant And His Attorney Of Record, Alison Crane, In The Amount Of $1,029.50
Off Calendar Feb-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Courtney m. Brown, Esq., Telephone 415-371-9114, appearing for Plaintiff;
Maria Nozzoino, Esq., Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane, LLP, Telephone 415-981-5411, appearing for Defendant Robery Heagy;

Parties met and confered in person before the start of today hearing outside the Courtroom.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS FUMIE GOLDWATER, MICHAEL GOLDWATER'S Motion To Compel Defendant Robert Heagys Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 15.1; Request For Sanctons Against Defendant And His Attorney Of Record, Alison Crane, In The Amount Of $1,029.50

Motion is denied as moot. Request for sanctions is denied. Prevailing party is to submit form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 by email to Judge Pro Tem Katherin Huibonhoa at Khuibonhoa@chjllp.com

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
